title: ring fails on a Nat truncated-subtraction cancellation despite a true hypothesis
informal_statement: For a b : ℕ with b ≤ a, the goal `a - b + b = a` attacked by `ring` alone, ignoring the hypothesis `b ≤ a`.

failure.input_context_summary: Goal `a - b + b = a` over `a b : ℕ` with hypothesis `h : b ≤ a` in context after `intro a b h`.
failure.attempt_summary: Applied `ring` directly. `ℕ` is only a `CommSemiring` (no subtraction operation in the algebraic sense), so `ring` treats `Nat.sub` as an opaque atom and cannot consult the hypothesis `b ≤ a` to relate `a - b + b` to `a`; the kernel rejected the step with `unsolved goals`. Tracked via proofsearch episode b2f5f359-6f9c-42dd-b749-ee70b931ff70, step 1 (attempt dd173a71-b223-4f9c-be91-14686779720e), kernel_fail, diagnostic category `unsolved_goals`. The same episode then closed the obligation on step 2 with `omega`, which does understand truncated subtraction, reaching `kernel_verified`.
failure.lesson: Do not reach for `ring` on goals containing `ℕ` subtraction just because the shape looks like a ring identity — `ring` normalizes semiring operations only and ignores side-condition hypotheses like `b ≤ a`. Prefer `omega` (or `Nat.sub_add_cancel h`) for truncated-subtraction identities that depend on an ordering hypothesis.

notes: v0.1 negative example filling the algebra lane (previously empty). Produced through the tracked proof-search MCP loop: problem_version_id 550b86a3-5811-46ed-9780-e22dba2e1e70, episode_id b2f5f359-6f9c-42dd-b749-ee70b931ff70 (unsafe_dev_attestation dev mode). Step 1 (`ring`) recorded kernel_fail; step 2 (`omega`) recorded kernel_verified, terminating the episode with outcome kernel_verified / root_proved.
